content.problemStatement:
You're playing your favorite video game: Pekómon!

The objective of the game is, of course, to "Catch Them All".

There are NNN different Pekómon in the game. The iii-th of them has a catching difficulty of AiA_iAi​.

To catch these Pekómon, you must use Pekóballs. There are two types of Pekóballs available to you:


Normal Pekóballs, which cost XXX coins each.

You need to throw **exactly** AiA_iAi​ Normal Pekóballs to catch a Pekómon whose catching difficulty is AiA_iAi​.
Master Pekóballs, which cost YYY coins each.

You need to throw only 111 Master Pekóball to catch any Pekómon, no matter what its catching difficulty is.
Find the **minimum** number of coins you need to spend on buying Pekóballs, so that you can catch all NNN Pekómon.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N,X,N, X, N,X, and YYY — the number of Pekómon, the cost of a Normal Pekóball, and the cost of a Master Pekóball.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the catching difficulties of the Pekómon.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of coins you need to spend on buying Pekóballs, in order to catch all NNN Pekómon.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The catching difficulties of the four Pekómon are [4,5,2,3][4, 5, 2, 3][4,5,2,3].
It's best to just use Normal Pekóballs for all four of them.
The cost comes out to 5⋅(4+5+2+3)=705\cdot (4 + 5 + 2 + 3) = 705⋅(4+5+2+3)=70, and using less coins is not possible.
Test case 222: The catching difficulties of the four Pekómon are [4,5,2,3][4, 5, 2, 3][4,5,2,3].
This time, it's optimal to throw a Master Pekóball at the second Pekómon, and Normal Pekóballs at the rest.
The cost is 5⋅4+30+5⋅2+5⋅3=755\cdot 4 + 30 + 5\cdot 2 + 5\cdot 3 = 755⋅4+30+5⋅2+5⋅3=75.
Test case 333: It's optimal to use a Master Pekóball for the third, fourth, and fifth Pekómon, and Normal Pekóballs for the first two.
The cost is 3⋅11+3⋅23+100+100+100=4023\cdot 11 + 3\cdot 23 + 100 + 100 + 100 = 4023⋅11+3⋅23+100+100+100=402.
